skill.name: cpp-pro
skill.description: Guides modern C++ development with RAII, smart pointers, STL algorithms, templates, concurrency, testing, and performance practices.
skill.summary: Improve C++ code quality with modern language features, memory safety practices, and performance-focused guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static external command finding is a false positive caused by Markdown inline code around a documentation path. The weak cryptography finding is also a false positive; the referenced line describes C++ smart pointers, not a cryptographic algorithm. No prompt injection, exfiltration, executable scripts, or malicious intent were found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Inline Code
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 26 uses backticks to format a documentation path in Markdown. It does not execute a shell command and does not introduce command injection risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains only Markdown inline code around a path reference. No command interpreter, shell syntax, or user-controlled command construction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 3 describes smart pointers as a C++ memory management concept. It does not reference MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected text is part of the skill description and refers to C++ smart pointers. There is no cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or security-sensitive implementation.

content.value_statement: Modern C++ projects often need safer ownership, clearer templates, and better performance discipline. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code apply practical C++ guidance across refactoring, testing, and optimization work.
actual_capabilities: Guides use of modern C++ standards from C++11 through C++23.
Recommends RAII, smart pointers, and Rule of Zero practices for safer ownership.
Supports refactoring around templates, move semantics, STL containers, and STL algorithms.
Advises on CMake setup, header structure, unit tests, sanitizers, and benchmarks.
Provides performance-oriented review guidance using profiling and benchmark practices.
limitations: It provides guidance only and does not include executable tools or automation scripts.
It cannot prove correctness without compiling, testing, and profiling the target project.
It focuses on C++ and does not cover unrelated language ecosystems.
It does not provide a full project-specific architecture review without user-supplied code.

use_cases.title: Refactor Legacy Ownership
use_cases.description: Replace manual lifetime management with clearer RAII and smart pointer patterns.

use_cases.title: Review Performance-Critical Code
use_cases.description: Evaluate container choices, move semantics, algorithm use, and benchmark strategy.

use_cases.title: Set C++ Project Standards
use_cases.description: Create consistent expectations for CMake, headers, tests, sanitizers, and documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Modernize a Class
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this C++ class for modern C++ improvements. Focus on RAII, const correctness, smart pointers, and simple STL replacements.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Ownership
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this C++ module for ownership and lifetime risks. Recommend Rule of Zero, unique_ptr, shared_ptr, or stack allocation where appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Build and Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a CMake and test strategy for this C++ project. Include the C++ standard, unit tests, sanitizers, and benchmark coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Advanced Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this performance-sensitive C++ code. Consider move semantics, STL algorithms, concurrency, exception safety, profiling, and benchmark validation.

output_examples.input: Review a class that manually deletes heap objects.
output: Identifies ownership ambiguity and manual lifetime risks.
Recommends stack allocation or unique ownership where possible.
Suggests tests and sanitizer checks to validate the refactor.

output_examples.input: Improve a C++ library build setup.
output: Proposes a modern CMake structure with an explicit C++ standard.
Adds guidance for unit tests, include boundaries, and sanitizer runs.
Recommends benchmark coverage for performance-sensitive paths.
Highlights unclear template interfaces and compile-time diagnostics.
Suggests concepts or constraints when supported by the target standard.
Documents tradeoffs between generic design and readability.

best_practices: Provide the target C++ standard, compiler, platform, and build system before asking for changes.
Ask for validation steps, including tests, sanitizers, and benchmarks, when code behavior matters.
Share ownership expectations and performance constraints so recommendations fit the project.
anti_patterns: Do not request broad rewrites without providing build, test, and compatibility constraints.
Do not use the skill as a replacement for compiling and running the changed code.
Do not apply performance advice without measuring the relevant workload.

faq.question: What C++ standards does this skill cover?
faq.answer: It covers modern C++ guidance from C++11 through C++23, depending on the project constraints.

faq.question: Can it help with memory safety?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes RAII, stack allocation, smart pointers, and ownership clarity.

faq.question: Does it generate complete build files?
faq.answer: It can advise on CMake structure and expected settings, but the final build must be tested in the project.

faq.question: Can it optimize high-performance C++?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review algorithms, move semantics, containers, concurrency, profiling, and benchmark plans.

faq.question: Does it include executable scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill file contains guidance and does not include executable automation.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
